channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Michigan's Arab American community offers muted response to Trump's Gaza takeover plan
item.description: DEARBORN, Mich. (AP) — Residents of the largest Arab American community in the U.S. had plenty to say during the 2024 presidential campaign about the roiling politics in the Middle East.
item.dc:creator: JOEY CAPPELLETTI and MIKE HOUSEHOLDER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Walz sets special election for seat in suburban Ramsey County that will decide House power
item.description: A special election will fill a seat won by a Democrat in the November election that came open after a residency challenge. The result could return the Minnesota House to a tie or boost a GOP majority.
item.dc:creator: Dana Ferguson, MPR News

item.title: With Gaza rehab and other global policy ideas, Trump goes from America First to America Everywhere
item.description: President Donald Trump promised voters an administration that wouldn’t waste precious American lives and taxpayer treasure on far-off wars and nation building.
item.dc:creator: Aamer Madhani And Zeke Miller, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Guatemala agrees to accept deportees from other countries, in deal with Rubio
item.description: Guatemala has agreed to accept flights of illegal immigrants from the U.S., Secretary of State Marco Rubio announced Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: What Simon says in House goes, but temporary chamber leader tiptoes through intensely partisan terrain
item.description: Secretary of State Steve Simon is in the center of a fight over power in the Minnesota House of Representatives. The executive branch officeholder who was first elected to public office two decades ago says he’s not taking sides. Republicans have sued Simon and vice versa.
item.dc:creator: Clay Masters, MPR News

item.title: Trump to sign order banning trans athletes from women's sports
item.description: The executive order will ban people biologically assigned males at birth from competing in women's sports.
item.dc:creator: Libbey Dean, NewsNation

item.title: Senate tees up final vote on Trump OMB nominee Russell Vought
item.description: The Senate on Wednesday teed up a final vote on the nomination of Russell Vought to lead the White House budget office.
item.dc:creator: Aris Folley, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Judge cements block on Trump’s order restricting birthright citizenship
item.description: GREENBELT, Md. — A federal judge Wednesday furthered a block of President Trump’s executive order preventing the children of migrants without legal status from receiving birthright citizenship. U.S.
item.dc:creator: Zach Schonfeld, NewsNation

item.title: Army recruiting hits record highs after Trump's win
item.description: Officials say December 2024 was their best December in 15 years, with nearly 350 soldiers enlisted every day that month.
item.dc:creator: Elina Shirazi, NewsNation

item.title: Bill would allow schools to opt out of enrolling undocumented students
item.description: Senate Democratic leadership responded to the bill Tuesday, calling it "unconstitutional" and "prejudiced.
item.dc:creator: Alicia Patton, NewsNation

item.title: Senate Dems ask Hegseth to probe potential price gouging on military families' housing
item.description: “The Department of Defense has a responsibility to protect military families from predatory private housing companies.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, NewsNation

item.title: Shutdown fears grow as Trump battles multiply
item.description: The odds of a government shutdown are surging as President Trump battles Democrats over efforts to freeze funding and Republicans brawl internally over the size and scope of potential cuts.
item.dc:creator: Aris Folley, NewsNation

item.title: Long before RFK Jr., Brainerd fought bitter legal battle over fluoride in its water supply
item.description: The topic of whether to fluoridate drinking water is back in the public sphere again, in part because of the nomination of Robert F. Kennedy Jr. as Health and Human Services Secretary.
item.dc:creator: Kirsti Marohn, MPR News

item.title: Judge blocks transfers of 3 transgender inmates to men's prison
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — A federal judge agreed Tuesday to temporarily block prison officials from transferring three incarcerated transgender women to men’s facilities and terminating their access to hormone therapy under an executive order signed by President Donald Trump. U.S.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL KUNZELMAN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: FBI agents sue over Justice Dept. effort to ID employees involved in Trump-related investigations
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — FBI agents who participated in investigations related to President Donald Trump have sued over Justice Department efforts to develop a list of employees involved in those inquiries that they fear could be a precursor to mass firings.
item.dc:creator: ERIC TUCKER and ALANNA DURKIN RICHER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: FBI gives Justice details on employees who worked Jan. 6 cases, FBI agents sue
item.description: The details on FBI employees comes in response to a Justice Department memo last week, asking for names of all current and former FBI personnel who worked on Jan. 6 cases or the prosecution of Hamas leaders.
item.dc:creator: Ryan Lucas, MPR News

item.title: A guide to what the U.S. Education Department does (and doesn't) do
item.description: The department tracks student achievement, manages college financial aid and sends K-12 schools money to support students with disabilities and lower-income communities, among other things.
item.dc:creator: Nicole Cohen, Jonaki Mehta, Elissa Nadworny , and Cory Turner, MPR News

item.title: Race for NYC mayor heats up as primary opponents hit Adams on education, immigrants
item.description: ALBANY, N.Y. (PIX11) -- During a budget hearing in front of the state legislature Tuesday, two Democrats looking to unseat fellow Democratic Mayor Eric Adams took their shot. State Sen.
item.dc:creator: Henry Rosoff, PIX 11

item.title: Nevada prisons director points to 4-mile fence, better staffing; inmate food issues persist
item.description: A high-profile inmate escape, the resignation of the prisons director, and reports that fences, doors and windows were vulnerable. It all added up to a spotlight on the Nevada Department of Corrections in 2022.
item.dc:creator: Greg Haas, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Congress puts hold on Trump's $1 billion arms sale to Israel
item.description: Two congressional aides told The Hill on Tuesday that a hold has been placed on the arms sale package.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, NewsNation

item.title: Target is sued for 'defrauding' shareholders about DEI
item.description: Target has been sued for allegedly concealing the risks of its diversity and social initiatives, leading to a backlash that caused customers to flee and the stock price of the Minneapolis-based retailer to plummet.
item.dc:creator: MPR News

item.title: Trump wants US to take ownership over Gaza
item.description: After talks with Netanyahu, Trump suggested the U.S. "take over" Gaza and handle cleanup in the war-torn region.
item.dc:creator: Robert Sherman, NewsNation

item.title: Trump administration launches investigation into U of M on antisemitism reports
item.description: The U.S. Department of Education announced Monday it is opening investigations into five universities – including the University of Minnesota – where the agency says “widespread antisemitism harassment” has been reported.
item.dc:creator: Nicole Ki, MPR News

item.title: Nassau police to help ICE detain undocumented immigrants accused of crimes
item.description: NASSAU COUNTY, N.Y. (PIX11) – The Nassau County Police Department will be directly participating in ICE enforcement to detain undocumented immigrants accused of crimes, County Executive Bruce Blakeman announced on Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: Erin Pflaumer, PIX 11

item.title: Trump oil tariffs expected to raise price at the pump
item.description: President Trump’s tariff on Canadian oil is expected to raise prices at the pump for U.S. consumers, if it takes effect as planned after a 30-day delay.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Frazin, Fox 5

item.title: El Salvador offers to accept deportees from US of any nationality, including Americans: Rubio
item.description: The U.S. government cannot deport American citizens and such a move would be met with significant legal challenges.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Schools poised to be major front line in Trump immigration crackdown
item.description: Oklahoma is moving to require proof of citizenship for the guardians of public school students, while California schools are distributing "know your rights" cards to immigrant families as K-12 campuses become a crucial front line in President Trump's immigration crackdown.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, Fox 5

item.title: Cannabis business license applications to open in February
item.description: Minnesota’s Office of Cannabis Management said it will start accepting applications for business licenses in two weeks. It’s the next step in the state’s rocky launch of a legal cannabis market.
item.dc:creator: Estelle Timar-Wilcox, MPR News

item.title: Local congressman reacts to inspectors general dismissed by President Trump
item.description: In the flurry of Trump's executive orders covering immigration raids, federal firings, funding freezes and tariff negotiations, the dismissing of 17 inspector generals has gotten little notice, but according to San Diego Congressman Mike Levin, this is a bigger issue than most understand.
item.dc:creator: Jaime Chambers, Fox 5

item.title: Nevada Senate was a battleground in 2023, new Republican minority leader emphasizes setting priorities
item.description: The Nevada Senate is ready to get down to business, and the end of the 2023 Legislature is still fresh in the minds of Democrats and Republicans gathered in Carson City.
item.dc:creator: John Langeler, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Nevada Legislature observes 'family day,' targets high price of eggs as session starts
item.description: State lawmakers convened in Carson City on Monday, beginning the 83rd Legislative Session with the legislative chambers filled with family and friends.
item.dc:creator: Greg Haas, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: NJ bill looks to give veteran nonprofits a boost
item.description: UNION, N.J. (PIX11) - Almost any veteran non-profit organization would tell you that more money means more critical services they can provide to those who've served our country. New Jersey Assemblywoman Tennille McCoy is authoring a bill that would hope to give certain veteran nonprofits a hand.
item.dc:creator: Jim Vasil, PIX 11

item.title: Dozens of Education Department staffers on leave amid Trump's DEI purge
item.description: Dozens of employees with the Department of Education were placed on administrative leave on Friday, allegedly after attending a diversity training course encouraged by then-Secretary Betsy DeVos during the first Trump administration.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, Fox 5

item.title: President Donald Trump agrees to pause tariffs on Mexico
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Mexican President Claudia Sheinbaum said Monday that after a conversation with U.S. President Donald Trump that the planned tariffs are on hold for a month, a statement confirmed by the White House.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: How non-violent undocumented immigrants could be swept up in raids
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) – In two weeks of the second Trump administration, the White House is staying true to tough immigration policies with ICE raids across the country. Several have happened in the tri-state area.
item.dc:creator: Dan Mannarino, PIX 11

item.title: Who is Transportation Secretary Sean Duffy? You may recognize him from MTV
item.description: Duffy, formerly a reality TV star and Wisconsin congressman, was confirmed as the new transportation secretary on Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: Jill Colvin, Fox 5

item.title: Senate Democrats revolt against Trump in fallout from foreign aid freeze
item.description: Senate Democrats are starting to revolt against President Trump’s agenda on Capitol Hill amid fallout from an initial freeze on all foreign assistance, warning Republicans that failure to work together will incur consequences. Sen. Chris Coons (D-Del.) on Thursday reversed support for Rep.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, Fox 5
